name: Anthony Jackson
description: American electric bass guitar player, session musician, songwriter
abstract: Anthony Claiborne Jackson (June 23, 1952 – October 19, 2025) was an American bassist. Described as "one of the masters of the instrument", he performed as a session musician and live artist.

concerts.label: Tania Maria, Singer, with Michel Camilo, Piano

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: We did – I did a record with him a couple years ago with Steve Smith and Anthony Jackson called Flashback [Flashpoint].
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Paquito D'Rivera
about_quotes.evidence: for one note, we have Jon Faddis, Victor Paz, Anthony Jackson, and Stan Getz... I can’t believe I am here to play one note with the most, the most famous musicians in the world
about_quotes.doc_title: Paquito D'Rivera — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker
about_quotes.evidence: And my side is with a great bass player Anthony Jackson and we had worked together a lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chris Parker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 5, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: I spent five years with the Horace Silver Quintet... Anthony Jackson — I’m trying to think of all the people who were in Horace’s band when I was in there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: Anthony Jackson was famous because he’d say “flaw” and he’d stop a take... But Anthony wanted it perfect.
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: The bass player he really loved was Anthony Jackson, electric bass player. He really loved Anthony.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
